strengths: Effective use of communication ATL skills: interview data and email exchanges enrich primary research and demonstrate impactful dialogue with peers and supervisors
Strong self-management: tasks broken into manageable steps using Asana, with deadlines and status tracking to sustain progress
Proficient information-literacy: well-focused guiding questions and thorough research process showcase targeted inquiry
Digital-tool mastery: web-based notepad for materials tracking ensures resource oversight and reduces errors
weaknesses: Connections between tool usage (Asana, notepad) and specific improvements in the product are not explicitly stated
Descriptions of supervisor meetings lack concrete examples of how feedback led to product refinements
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You have applied a wide array of ATL skillsâcommunication, self-management and information-literacyâin ways that clearly support your progress. To further elevate this section, explicitly link each tool or strategy to measurable outcomes (for instance, note how an Asana reminder prevented a missed prototype deadline) and cite exact changes made after supervisory feedback to illustrate the direct impact of your interactions.

strengths: Compelling personal-interest narrative that grounds the project in heritage concerns, giving the work strong motivation and authenticity
Well-structured action plan with a clear breakdown of tasks, reflecting thoughtful sequencing toward the intended product
weaknesses: Success criteria remain broad and lack measurable thresholds, making objective evaluation difficult
Design-specifications table is incompleteâtesting methods are missing for several specifications, compromising feasibility checks
Several cells in the action plan (justifications and time estimates) are empty, reducing clarity on how tasks align with criteria and deadlines
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates a strong conceptual foundation through a vivid personal narrative and a logical breakdown of tasks. To strengthen this section, refine each success criterion with specific, quantifiable targets (for example, âretain heat for 30 minutesâ) and complete the missing fields in both the design-specifications table and the action plan to ensure every requirement can be tested and tracked unambiguously.

strengths: Insightful user-centred reflection on size trade-offs and heat-retention trade-offs, leading to purposeful design adjustments
Strong environmental awareness: identification of non-biodegradable elements with concrete proposals for sustainable alternatives
Use of a personalized rubric and candid self-ratings demonstrates rigorous self-evaluation and metacognitive depth
Connections to IB learner-profile attributes (âBalanced,â âReflectiveâ) show clear self-awareness and articulated personal growth
weaknesses: Reflection does not systematically evaluate the final product against each success criterion due to the criteriaâs lack of specificity
Impact on personal learning could be supported with more detailed evidence (for example, quantifiable improvements in knitting speed or precision)
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflections are rich and multifaceted, showing strong environmental, functional and personal learning insights. To deepen this section, revisit each defined success criterion (once they are quantified) and evaluate your productâs performance against them. Additionally, bolster your discussion of learning impact with specific metricsâsuch as improvements in knitting time or error reductionâto provide objective evidence of your growth.

annotations.comment: Including data from your interview with a skilled peer enriches your primary research and demonstrates authentic application of communication ATL skills.
You describe communication with supervisors but stop short of linking decisions from those meetings to specific product changes. Cite concrete examples of adjustments that resulted.
You clearly explain how composing emails and conducting meetings improved both digital and interpersonal communication, illustrating strong ATL skill development.
Your organization-skills section cites tools (Asana, online notepad) but doesnât specify how each tool directly improved your product process. Link tool usage to outcomes.
Breaking down tasks into manageable steps demonstrates excellent self-management. This approach supports sustained progress and efficient execution.
Using a web-based notepad for materials tracking shows effective use of digital tools to streamline resource management and ensure nothing is overlooked.
Linking your experiences to IB learner-profile attributes âBalancedâ and âReflectiveâ shows clear metacognition and self-awareness in your project journey.
Your description of Asana usageâsetting deadlines, tracking status, and prioritizing tasksâdemonstrates proactive time management and accountability.
Your nuanced discussion of size trade-offsâhandling ease for large vs. capacity for smallâshows user-centred reflection and insightful design refinement ideas.
Acknowledging non-biodegradable elements (resin, beads) and proposing biodegradable alternatives highlights strong reflective thinking on environmental impact.
Your reflection vividly captures how the project fostered perseverance, creativity, and patienceâkey personal-growth outcomes that extend beyond this product.
Your identification of heat-retention gaps in the knitting technique and recommendation to adjust stitch density shows precise functional evaluation.
Analyzing how the hotpot resonated (or didnât) with your target audience demonstrates deep reflection on design outreach and scalability.
Several rows in your design-specifications table lack testing-method entries. Complete the testing methods for every specification to ensure feasibility and clear validation steps.
Your success criteria explain why benchmarks are useful but lack quantifiable details. Include measurable thresholds (e.g., âretain heat for 30 minutesâ) to evaluate each criterion objectively.
Identifying the scarcity of wheat-husk crafting sources demonstrates critical reflection on research challenges and justifies your reliance on community knowledge.

Your personal interest narrative clearly establishes a motivating context and demonstrates a compelling connection between heritage concerns and your learning goal. This strong rationale strengthens your projectâs focus and authenticity.

The intended product description is clear but the success criteria here are broad. Define specific, measurable targets (e.g., number of prototypes knitted, heat-retention duration) to track your productâs success.

Your action plan table shows strong task breakdown but several justification and time cells are empty. Fill in all fields to clarify how each short-term goal aligns with specific success criteria and deadlines.
You present a thorough description of your research process and its role in achieving your learning goal. This demonstrates effective application of information-literacy ATL skills.
Your experimental approach to optimizing drying methods shows resourceful problem-solving and iterative improvement in material preparation.
Balancing aesthetics with functionality through color-theory research and bead-pattern tests demonstrates a nuanced evaluation of design trade-offs.
Regularly revisiting your timeline to adjust priorities shows strong adaptive time-management and reflective practice.
Your guiding questions are focused and relevant, steering your research toward cultural relevance, youth engagement, and environmental responsibility.
Starting with simpler materials to build knitting skills before applying to wheat husks demonstrates thoughtful skill-development sequencing.
Your use of your own rubric for self-evaluation shows alignment between planned criteria and observed evidence, strengthening the rigour of your reflections.
Candidly rating your bead-arrangement challenges (6/8) and noting areas for securing beads demonstrates honest evaluation and sets a clear path for improvement.
The comparison of knife-grip versus pencil-grip for needle handling is a precise example of applying literacy skills to optimize your knitting efficiency.
